scope.summary: Scoped reconciliation close of the Cluster F (§28 bug-hunt) parent: all direct Cluster F children are closed in Beads; all oraclemcp-owned F-LOW descendants are closed with bound evidence; the sole open descendant, DC5, is explicitly recorded as a rust-oracledb driver handoff outside this checkout.
out_of_scope: Implementing DC5: bead oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.1 targets /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs and remains open for the driver pane.
Re-opening or re-verifying the already-closed children individually; their own close reasons and evidence artifacts remain the source of truth.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib held_execute_survives_late_cancellation_and_is_not_reported_as_cancelled
entry_points.trace: F-DI1 (bp8ia.7.8) discriminating pin: held oracle_execute is exactly-once; a late outer deadline no longer discards the successful response and a retry cannot double-apply the DML. Implementing commit 2e54c6a9 is an ancestor of source 7ce64039. Re-run 2026-07-22 with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target: 1 passed, 0 failed.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp-telemetry --lib tool_label_cardinality_is_bounded_against_attacker_controlled_names
entry_points.trace: F-MET (bp8ia.7.9) discriminating pin: N attacker-controlled tool names yield O(1) distinct metric labels using the advertised-tools allowlist and unlisted sentinel, not O(N). Implementing commit 549fc8e6 is an ancestor of source 7ce64039. Re-run 2026-07-22 with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target: 1 passed, 0 failed.

entry_points.name: git merge-base --is-ancestor 2e54c6a9 7ce64039 / 549fc8e6 7ce64039 / 124d62c1 7ce64039
entry_points.trace: All three server-side implementing commits (DI1=2e54c6a9, MET=549fc8e6, DI6 terminal-effect=124d62c1) are ancestors of the source commit.

entry_points.name: tracker parent-child descendant query
entry_points.trace: The exported Beads graph at source 7ce64039 has 17 direct parent-child children for oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7, all status=closed. Its F-LOW normalization child has 20 descendants: 19 status=closed and 1 status=open. The open descendant is DC5, a driver-side bead explicitly handed off in comments 111 and 139; all oraclemcp-owned Cluster F descendants are closed.

entry_points.name: DC5 handoff audit
entry_points.trace: br show oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.1 --json shows status=open, labels include repo-driver, and comment 139 records the active blocker: the implementation site is the sibling rust-oracledb repo, no rust-oracledb edit/test/commit was made from this oraclemcp-only pane, and the fix remains routed to the driver pane.

limitations: tree_clean is computed over the scoped paths only; the wider shared checkout carries other agents' uncommitted work on unrelated files (constitution rule 13).
DC5 is not claimed fixed or closed here. It remains open in this oraclemcp tracker as a driver-side handoff for /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs.
Driver-side §28 findings are verified only via their own tracker closes or handoff comments, not re-run here; the sibling driver checkout is outside this pane's editing scope.
This is a scoped-test basis: the two cargo pins confirm the server fixes; they do not re-certify the full Cluster F acceptance across both repositories.

known_defects.description: DC5 remains open for the driver pane: sub-minute FixedOffset values still need typed rejection in rust-oracledb.

follow_ups: oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.1: driver-side DC5 handoff remains open.
